operating_state: Total revenues increased 15% to $402.8 billion driven by Google Services growth and Google Cloud acceleration. Google Search revenues rose from increased queries and advertiser spending, while YouTube ads grew across direct response and brand products. Google Cloud maintained robust enterprise adoption momentum with infrastructure and platform services expansion. Revenue backlog reached $242.8 billion with over 50% expected recognition within 24 months.
cost_structure: Cost of revenues increased 11% from higher content acquisition costs, depreciation expense, and traffic acquisition costs. Operating expenses surged 22% driven by employee compensation increases including stock-based compensation, Waymo valuation charge, and European Commission fine. Capital expenditures increased to $91.4 billion from $52.5 billion supporting AI-optimized infrastructure including custom TPU development and data center expansion.
material_events: European Commission imposed $3.5 billion fine for advertising technology practices, appealed by Google. Entered definitive agreements to acquire Wiz and Intersect, both expected to close in 2026. Executed $45.4 billion in share repurchases and increased quarterly dividend 5% to $0.21 per share. Waymo announced $16.0 billion investment round majority funded by Alphabet. Launched Gemini 3 multimodal AI model with enhanced reasoning capabilities.
macro_exposures: Supply chain constraints affecting specialized AI chip manufacturing and server equipment from limited qualified suppliers with extended disruptions potentially impacting demand fulfillment. Energy supply limitations globally constraining data center expansion due to increased AI compute requirements and limited power, water, and land resources. Geopolitical tensions including Middle East and Ukraine conflicts creating operational disruptions and cybersecurity risks.
forward_outlook: Management expects significant increases in technical infrastructure investment for 2026 relative to 2025, particularly servers, network equipment, and data centers supporting AI offerings. Strategic focus on scaling Other Bets commercialization while maintaining significant R&D investment across transportation and life sciences verticals. Authorized additional $70.0 billion share repurchase program with commitment to continued quarterly dividend payments.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple antitrust proceedings including DOJ advertising technology remedies determination with structural remedy proposals that could materially impact operations. Unresolved European Court of Justice appeals for Android and AdSense decisions with outstanding fines under appeal. Intensifying AI regulatory framework implementation across jurisdictions creates compliance obligations. Unrecognized tax benefits totaled $13.1 billion with ongoing examinations across multiple jurisdictions.
